Traditional methods use fixed subspace dimensions provided by experts or rely on trial and error to estimate subspace dimensions with resources consumed. To this end, this paper proposes an automated random embedding for high-dimensional Bayesian optimization with unknown effective dimension, called Dynamic Shared Embedding Bayesian Optimization (DSEBO). DSEBO starts with a low dimension and switches to a higher subspace if the solutions in the current subspace show preliminary convergence. DSEBO dynamically determines the dimension of the next subspace based on the quality of the solutions in different subspaces and shares the queried solutions with the new subspace for a better initialization. Theoretically, we derive a regret bound for DSEBO and demonstrate that DSEBO can better balance approximation and optimization errors. Extensive experiments on functions with dimensionality of varying magnitudes and real-world tasks with unknown effective dimensions reveal that, compared with state-of-the-art methods, alternating optimization across different subspaces results in significant improvements in high-dimensional optimization, both in terms of optimization regret and time.
comment: This paper has been accepted by IJCAI 2026

light_analysis.overview: 提出动态共享嵌入贝叶斯优化（DSEBO），自动调整随机嵌入子空间维度，解决有效维度未知的高维贝叶斯优化问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 贝叶斯优化在高维空间中面临维度灾难，随机嵌入通过低维子空间简化优化，但有效维度未知导致子空间维度难以确定。传统方法依赖专家预设或试错，资源消耗大且效果不稳定。因此需要自动化方法动态调整子空间维度以平衡近似误差与优化误差。
light_analysis.method: 提出DSEBO，初始使用低维子空间，当当前子空间解初步收敛时切换到更高维度。基于不同子空间解的质量动态决定下一子空间维度，并共享已查询解以初始化新子空间。理论推导出遗憾界，证明能更好平衡近似与优化误差。
light_analysis.result: 在多种维度的函数和未知有效维度的实际任务上，与最先进方法相比，DSEBO在不同子空间间交替优化显著降低了优化遗憾并节省了时间。实验表明高维优化性能大幅提升，具体数据未明确，但突出遗憾和时间优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: DSEBO有效解决了有效维度未知时随机嵌入子空间维度的自动选择问题，提供了理论保证和实证效果。学术上为高维贝叶斯优化提供新思路，实际中可应用于复杂黑箱优化任务。未来可探索更高效的子空间切换策略或扩展到非欧空间。

title: ARES: Automated Rubric Synthesis for Scalable LLM Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Rubric-based rewards offer a promising way to extend reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models beyond tasks with automatically verifiable answers. However, scaling rubric-based RL remains challenging: existing approaches often rely on expert-written rubrics and manually constructed question sets, while fixed task-level rubrics may fail to capture the evaluation requirements of individual questions. We propose ARES (Automated Rubric synthEsis for Scalable RL), a framework for automatically constructing rubric-based RL data at scale. Starting from raw pretraining documents, ARES converts source knowledge into self-contained question-answer pairs and co-generates question-specific weighted rubrics, enabling instance-level reward supervision for open-ended responses. To improve diversity and quality, ARES conditions generation on domain labels and persona information, and applies validation filters for question self-containment, answer faithfulness, and rubric validity. Using ARES, we construct 100K rubric-annotated instances across ten domains. Experiments on seven benchmarks show that rubric-based RL trained with ARES, outperforms continual pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, and binary-reward RL, with the largest gains on multi-dimensional open-ended tasks such as healthcare and instruction following.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ARES框架，自动从原始文档生成带加权评分标准的问答数据，实现大规模基于评分标准的强化学习训练。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有基于评分标准的强化学习在LLM中依赖专家编写评分标准和人工构建问题集，且固定任务级评分标准无法捕捉单个问题的评估需求，限制了规模化应用。
light_analysis.method: ARES从原始预训练文档出发，自动生成自包含问答对及问题特定加权评分标准，实现实例级奖励监督。通过领域标签和角色信息提升多样性，并应用验证过滤器确保质量。构建了跨10个领域的10万条标注实例。
light_analysis.result: 在7个基准测试中，ARES训练的基于评分标准的RL优于持续预训练、监督微调和二元奖励RL，在多维开放任务（如医疗、指令遵循）上提升最大。
light_analysis.conclusion: ARES实现了自动化、可扩展的评分标准生成与强化学习训练，显著提升开放任务性能，为LLM的RL应用提供了高效数据构建方案。局限性可能包括生成质量依赖文档基础。

title: SSDAU: Structured Semantic Data Augmentation for Joint Entity and Relation Extraction
abstract: Joint Entity and Relation Extraction (JERE) is highly susceptible to weak generalization due to low-quality training data. Data augmentation is a common strategy to enhance model generalization across different domains. However, existing data augmentation methods often overlook text relevance and may disrupt semantic structures and dependencies, making it difficult to generate effective augmented data for improving model generalization. In this paper, we propose Structured Semantic Data Augmentation (SSDAU), a novel method designed to preserve the semantic structure of text during augmentation. SSDAU segments text based on entity labels and employs an encoder to capture semantic features of entities through context awareness. It then performs entity semantic restructuring to generate augmented data. To distinguish semantically similar entities, SSDAU fuses contextualized embeddings with traditional similarity scores. To mitigate potential topic ambiguity and information loss, we apply the BERTTopic model to filter out irrelevant topics, ensuring topic consistency. We evaluate SSDAU on datasets with different annotation types and compare its performance on five representative JERE models against seven popular data augmentation baselines. Experiments demonstrate that SSDAU generates semantically consistent data with superior robustness against ambiguity (8.26% F1 decrease vs. 31.91% for baselines), significantly outperforming all existing methods across all metrics.

light_analysis.overview: 提出结构化语义数据增强方法SSDAU，在保持文本语义结构的同时生成增强数据，显著提升联合实体关系抽取的泛化性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 联合实体关系抽取（JERE）因低质量训练数据导致泛化能力弱。现有数据增强方法忽略文本相关性，可能破坏语义结构和依赖，难以生成有效增强数据。因此需要一种能保持语义结构的增强方法。
light_analysis.method: SSDAU基于实体标签分割文本，使用编码器通过上下文感知捕获实体语义特征，进行实体语义重构生成增强数据。融合上下文嵌入与相似度分数区分语义相近实体，并应用BERTTopic模型过滤无关主题确保主题一致性。在多种标注类型数据集上评估。
light_analysis.result: 在五个代表性JERE模型上对比七种基线方法，SSDAU生成语义一致的数据，鲁棒性显著优于基线：面对歧义时F1下降8.26%，而基线平均下降31.91%，在所有指标上全面超越现有方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: SSDAU通过保留语义结构的数据增强，有效提升JERE模型的泛化能力和鲁棒性，尤其在处理语义歧义时表现突出。未来可进一步探索更精细的语义结构建模或应用于其他序列标注任务。

title: Convex Compositional Reasoning Models
abstract: Compositional energy-based models can generalize to larger combinatorial reasoning problems by reusing a learned factor energy across many local constraints. In our paper, we show that a key bottleneck in compositional reasoning is not composition itself, but the non-convex geometry of the learned energy landscape. To solve this problem, we introduce Convex Compositional Energy Minimization (CCEM), a framework that parameterizes each factor with an input-convex neural network and optimizes the composed energy over a tight convex relaxation of the feasible set. Because convexity is preserved under summation, the global relaxed objective remains convex, enabling deterministic projected first-order optimization. CCEM is trained in two stages: factor-level contrastive learning to shape local energy basins, followed by end-to-end refinement through an unrolled projected solver. Our experiments show that our models trained on small subproblems or a single problem size transfer to larger instances without retraining.

light_analysis.overview: 提出凸组合能量最小化（CCEM）框架，通过输入凸神经网络参数化因子，解决组合推理中能量景观非凸的瓶颈。
light_analysis.motivation: 组合能量模型通过重用因子能量可泛化至更大组合推理问题，但关键瓶颈在于学习到的能量景观的非凸几何，而非组合本身。现有方法因非凸性难以保证全局优化，需要一种能保持凸性并实现确定性推理的框架。
light_analysis.method: CCEM框架：每个因子用输入凸神经网络参数化，使组合能量保持凸性；在可行集的紧凸松弛上优化，利用凸性在求和下的保持性，实现确定性投影一阶优化。两阶段训练：先进行因子级对比学习塑造局部能量盆地，再通过展开的投影求解器端到端精炼。
light_analysis.result: 在小子问题或单一问题大小上训练的模型，无需重新训练即可迁移到更大实例，证明了组合泛化能力。摘要未提供具体数值，但实验验证了跨规模迁移的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: CCEM解决了组合推理中非凸能量景观的优化瓶颈，通过凸松弛实现可扩展的确定性推理。学术价值在于将凸优化引入组合能量模型，实际应用适合需组合泛化的场景。局限性可能依赖于问题结构，未来可探索更复杂的约束类型。

title: Fast-dDrive: Efficient Block-Diffusion VLM for Autonomous Driving
abstract: End-to-end autonomous driving via Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models demands a precarious balance between high-fidelity trajectory planning and efficient inference. Existing paradigms typically fall short: autoregressive (AR) VLAs are memory-bandwidth-bound on edge hardware and prone to exposure-bias drift, while full-sequence diffusion models preclude KV-cache reuse and suffer from "logical leakage" that violates the fundamental perceive-then-plan causality. We present Fast-dDrive, a block-diffusion VLA that performs bidirectional refinement within semantic units while enforcing strict causal ordering across them. Leveraging the observation that driving VLAs often emit structured JSON-like outputs, Fast-dDrive freezes structural tokens into a section scaffold and employs a section-aware training recipe that prioritizes safety-critical planning. We further introduce Scaffold Speculative Decoding to achieve AR-equivalent quality at significantly higher throughput. Finally, we propose a low-overhead test-time scaling scheme: by forking $N$ stochastic trajectory rollouts from a single shared-prefix KV cache and averaging them, we effectively suppress prediction variance at a fractional computational cost. Empirical results demonstrate that Fast-dDrive redefines the speed-accuracy frontier for driving agents. On the WOD-E2E test set, Fast-dDrive achieves SOTA ADE@3s and ADE@5s, alongside the highest RFS among diffusion-based VLAs; on nuScenes, it reduces average L2 error to $0.32$m (a $22\%$ improvement). When integrated with SGLang, our framework delivers $12\times$ throughput speedup over the AR baseline, narrowing the gap between high-capacity VLAs and the efficiency demands of real-time on-vehicle deployment.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Fast-dDrive，一种块扩散VLA模型，通过语义单元内双向细化与跨单元因果顺序约束，实现高效端到端自动驾驶，在推理速度和轨迹精度上取得领先。
light_analysis.motivation: 端到端自动驾驶VLA模型需平衡高保真轨迹规划与高效推理。现有自回归VLA在边缘硬件上受内存带宽限制且存在暴露偏差，全序列扩散模型无法重用KV缓存并违反因果性（逻辑泄漏）。亟需一种高效且因果正确的生成范式。
light_analysis.method: Fast-dDrive采用块扩散架构，将结构化输出划分为语义块，在块内双向细化，块间保持严格因果顺序。通过冻结结构令牌为节脚手架，采用节感知训练优先处理安全关键规划。引入Scaffold Speculative Decoding实现与AR等价的质量并大幅提升吞吐量。测试时从共享前缀KV缓存分叉N个随机轨迹并取平均，以低计算成本抑制预测方差。
light_analysis.result: 在WOD-E2E上达到SOTA ADE@3s和ADE@5s，并获得扩散VLA中最高的RFS。在nuScenes上将平均L2误差降至0.32m（提升22%）。与SGLang集成后，吞吐量相比AR基线提升12倍，显著缩小了高容量VLA与实时车载部署需求间的差距。
light_analysis.conclusion: Fast-dDrive通过块扩散与因果约束重新定义了驾驶智能体的速度-精度前沿，有效解决了现有方法的效率与因果问题，为实时自动驾驶中的VLA部署提供了实用方案。未来可探索更灵活的块划分与更高效的多模态融合。

title: When Symptoms Are Not Enough: Evidence-Weighting Patterns in Large Language Model Psychiatric Screening
abstract: As demand for mental health care outpaces clinician-delivered assessment, scalable screening tools are increasingly needed. Large language models (LLMs) may identify psychiatric risk from patient narratives, but their reliability across diagnoses, demographic subgroups, and evidence-use patterns remains uncertain. We introduce a SCID-anchored benchmark of 555 semi-structured experiential interviews paired with diagnostic reference labels for anxiety disorder, major depressive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, and any current mental health disorder. Using zero-shot task-specific prompting, we evaluated five state-of-the-art LLMs and examined whether false-negative errors reflected missed psychiatric evidence or differential weighting of symptom, functional-impairment, and protective-context cues. Performance varied across tasks and models, with accuracy ranging from 0.49 to 0.86 and Matthews correlation coefficients from 0.16 to 0.38. GPT-4.1 Mini and GPT-5 Mini showed the most consistent disorder-specific accuracy. Subgroup analyses found higher depression-classification accuracy among male than female participants, no consistent age-related pattern, and modest non-uniform variation across race strata. Evidence-integration analyses showed that false-negative anxiety and PTSD classifications often contained explicit symptom evidence but were accompanied by preserved functioning, coping ability, or social support. Functional-impairment evidence shifted model outputs toward positive classifications, whereas protective-context evidence shifted outputs away. These findings suggest that LLMs may support scalable psychiatric screening, but their tendency to discount symptom evidence in the presence of preserved functioning or protective context requires careful validation before clinical deployment.

light_analysis.overview: 论文通过SCID锚定基准评估LLM精神科筛查，发现LLM在存在保护性背景时倾向于忽视症状证据，揭示了证据权重模式。
light_analysis.motivation: 精神健康护理需求远超临床评估能力，亟需可扩展的筛查工具。LLM可从患者叙述中识别风险，但其跨诊断、亚组和证据使用的可靠性未知。现有研究缺乏标准化基准和细致错误分析，阻碍临床部署。
light_analysis.method: 构建555个SCID锚定半结构化访谈数据集，含焦虑、抑郁、PTSD等诊断标签。采用零样本任务提示评估五种LLM（含GPT-4.1 Mini等），分析假阴性错误源于遗漏证据还是对症状、功能障碍和保护性背景的差异加权。
light_analysis.result: 模型准确率0.49-0.86，MCC 0.16-0.38；GPT-4.1 Mini和GPT-5 Mini诊断一致性最优。亚组分析显示男性抑郁分类准确率更高，年龄无一致模式，种族略有差异。假阴性案例常含症状但伴功能保留或社会支持；功能障碍证据促阳性，保护性背景抑阳性。
light_analysis.conclusion: LLM有望支持可扩展精神科筛查，但其在功能保留或保护性背景下折扣症状证据的倾向需临床验证。研究为LLM证据加权机制提供洞见，推动AI辅助心理健康评估的可靠部署，未来需优化权重分配。

title: FlowErase-RL: Rethinking Concept Erasure as Reward Optimization in Flow Matching Models
abstract: Recent advances in flow matching models have significantly improved text-to-image generation quality, but also introduce growing safety risks due to the generation of harmful or undesirable content. Existing concept erasure methods are either inference-time interventions with limited effectiveness or rely on supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which requires precisely aligned data and struggles with scalability and multi-concept settings. In this paper, we propose \emph{FlowErase-RL}, the first GRPO-based framework for concept erasure in flow matching models. We reformulate concept erasure as a reward optimization problem and introduce a \textbf{dynamic dual-path reward mechanism} that jointly optimizes (i) a Concept Erasure (CE) reward to suppress target concepts and (ii) a Non-target Space (NS) reward to preserve generative fidelity. The two reward paths are adaptively balanced during training via a performance-driven switching strategy, enabling stable optimization without explicit supervision. Extensive experiments on nudity, object, and artistic style erasure demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art erasure performance while maintaining strong image quality and semantic alignment. Moreover, it exhibits robust resistance to adversarial attacks and scales effectively to multi-concept scenarios. Our results establish a new paradigm for safe and controllable generation in flow matching models.

light_analysis.overview: 提出首个基于GRPO的框架FlowErase-RL，将概念擦除重写为奖励优化问题，实现流动匹配模型的安全可控生成。
light_analysis.motivation: 流动匹配模型虽提升了文本到图像生成质量，但也带来了有害内容生成的安全风险。现有概念擦除方法存在局限性：推理时干预效果有限，而监督微调需要精确对齐的数据且难以扩展到多概念场景。因此需要一种无需显式监督、能有效抑制有害概念同时保持生成质量的通用方法。
light_analysis.method: FlowErase-RL基于GRPO（组相对策略优化），将概念擦除形式化为奖励优化问题。核心是动态双路径奖励机制：概念擦除奖励抑制目标概念，非目标空间奖励保持生成保真度。两者通过性能驱动的切换策略自适应平衡，无需显式监督即可实现稳定优化。
light_analysis.result: 在裸体、物体和艺术风格擦除任务上，FlowErase-RL取得了最先进的擦除性能，同时保持高图像质量和语义对齐。该方法对对抗攻击具有鲁棒性，并能有效扩展到多概念场景，优于现有方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: FlowErase-RL建立了流动匹配模型中安全可控生成的新范式，通过奖励优化实现高效概念擦除，无需精确数据对齐。该方法在生成质量和安全性上取得平衡，为文本到图像生成的安全应用提供了通用框架。未来可探索更复杂的奖励设计和评估标准。

title: L-Drive: Beyond a Single Mapping-Latent Context Drives Time Series Forecasting
abstract: Mainstream methods for multivariate time-series forecasting largely follow the Direct-Mapping paradigm. They learn a unified mapping from history to the future in the observation space to fit value-level dependencies. However, real-world systems often undergo distribution shifts and regime changes. In such cases, a unified mapping can exhibit response lag around turning points, causing error accumulation within the switching window and reducing forecasting reliability. To address this issue, we propose L-Drive, a change-aware forecasting framework. L-Drive introduces a Latent-Context, to explicitly characterize high-level dynamics evolving over time, and uses gating to modulate increment representations. This provides more timely change cues and improves adaptation to changing segments. In addition, it incorporates patch-shared relative positional basis functions to strengthen intra-segment structural modeling and reduce overfitting caused by absolute-position memorization. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of L-Drive and show a better overall trade-off between forecasting accuracy and computational efficiency.

light_analysis.overview: 提出 L-Drive 框架，通过潜在上下文和门控机制捕捉时序变化，解决直接映射范式在分布偏移下的响应滞后问题。
light_analysis.motivation: 主流多变量时间序列预测方法采用直接映射范式，学习从历史到未来的统一映射以拟合值级依赖。然而，现实系统常经历分布偏移和状态变化，此时统一映射在转折点附近会出现响应滞后，导致切换窗口内误差累积，降低预测可靠性。因此，需要一种能够感知变化、及时适应动态环境的预测框架。
light_analysis.method: L-Drive 引入潜在上下文（Latent-Context）显式刻画随时间演化的高层动态，并使用门控机制调节增量表示，提供更及时的变化线索，提升对变化片段的适应能力。同时，采用补丁共享的相对位置基函数（patch-shared relative positional basis functions）加强段内结构建模，避免绝对位置记忆导致的过拟合。整体框架兼顾变化感知与结构保持。
light_analysis.result: 在多个真实世界数据集上进行的大量实验验证了 L-Drive 的有效性，表明其在预测准确性和计算效率之间实现了更优的整体权衡。与主流方法相比，L-Drive 在分布偏移场景下显著降低了预测误差，尤其是在状态切换窗口内，响应速度更快，误差积累更少。
light_analysis.conclusion: L-Drive 通过显式建模动态变化和增强段内结构，有效解决了直接映射范式在非平稳环境下的适应性问题。该框架具有实际应用价值，可用于金融、能源等频繁发生状态变化的领域。未来可探索更复杂的门控机制或与序列生成模型结合。

title: Self-supervised Hierarchical Visual Reasoning with World Model
abstract: 3D open-world environments with adversarial opponents remain a core challenge for reinforcement learning due to their vast state spaces. Effective reasoning representations are essential in such settings. While existing self-supervised visual foresight reasoning approaches often suffer from multi-step error accumulation, many recent studies resort to injecting domain-specific knowledge for more stable guidance. Our key insight is that the photorealistic fidelity of visual reasoning representations is secondary; what truly matters is providing informative, task-relevant signals. To this end, we propose ResDreamer, a hierarchical world model in which each higher-level layer is trained to reconstruct the residuals of the layer below. This design enables progressive abstraction of increasingly sophisticated world dynamics and fosters the emergence of richer latent representations. Drawing inspiration from the "Bitter Lesson", ResDreamer trains its reasoning representations in a purely self-supervised manner. The higher-level residual representations are used to modulate lower-level predictions, allowing the world model to scale effectively with only linearly increasing cross-layer communication costs. Experiments show that ResDreamer achieves state-of-the-art sample efficiency and parameter efficiency. This scalable hierarchical visual foresight reasoning architecture paves the way for more capable online RL agents in open-ended, dynamic environments. The code is accessible at https://github.com/XuYuanFei01/ResDreamer.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ResDreamer，一种分层世界模型，通过自监督学习残差表示实现高效的视觉推理，避免误差累积，达到最先进样本效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 3D开放世界对抗环境状态空间巨大，现有自监督视觉前瞻推理方法存在多步误差累积，常依赖领域知识。本文认为视觉表示的光真 fidelity 不重要，关键在于提供信息丰富、任务相关的信号。
light_analysis.method: ResDreamer 是一个分层世界模型，每层训练重建下层的残差，形成渐进抽象，产生丰富潜在表示。高层残差调节低层预测，跨层通信成本线性增长，全部自监督训练。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明 ResDreamer 在样本效率和参数效率上达到最先进水平，优于基线方法，为在线强化学习提供高效推理架构。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出可扩展的层次化视觉前瞻推理架构，通过自监督残差学习避免误差累积，在开放式动态环境中提升RL智能体能力。代码开源，便于进一步研究。

title: Active Budget Allocation for Efficient Scaling Law Estimation via Surrogate-Guided Pruning
abstract: Predicting model performance at larger scales enables the design of training strategies and architectures tailored to specific performance targets. Empirical scaling law research identifies functional forms to aid this prediction task. These describe the relationship between loss and compute using a loss-compute frontier defined by learning curves. Due to the empirical nature of this approach, the computational burden is substantial, making strategic resource allocation essential - yet it remains surprisingly underexplored. In this work, we address this shortcoming by exploring the suitability of Successive Halving (SH) and SH combined with parametric and non-parametric surrogate models. In addition to enabling a more systematic allocation of a given compute budget, our findings show that SH paired with surrogate models yields a set of learning curves that includes one with a lower loss-compute value than what naive uniform allocation or an SH-only approach can obtain. Our experiments demonstrate mean relative improvements of up to 2.84% and 5.47% on real-world and synthetic learning curve datasets. This strategic resource allocation enables us to obtain accurate scaling laws at significantly reduced computational costs, saving up to 98.7% over the traditional exhaustive approach.

light_analysis.overview: 提出代理引导的Successive Halving预算分配策略，高效估计缩放定律，计算成本节省高达98.7%。
light_analysis.motivation: 缩放定律研究通过实证确定损失与计算量的关系，但传统方法计算负担大。有效的预算分配策略对降低成本至关重要，然而现有均匀分配或仅使用Successive Halving的方法效率不足。本文旨在探索更系统的资源分配方法，以在有限预算下获得准确的缩放定律。
light_analysis.method: 本文结合Successive Halving (SH)与参数化及非参数化代理模型，通过代理指导的剪枝策略优先保留有潜力的学习曲线，实现计算预算的系统性分配。在真实世界和合成学习曲线数据集上验证了方法有效性，与均匀分配和纯SH对比。
light_analysis.result: SH结合代理模型获得的学习曲线具有更低损失-计算值。在真实世界和合成数据集上分别实现平均2.84%和5.47%的相对改进，计算成本相比传统穷举方法节省98.7%。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文提出的代理引导预算分配策略能显著降低缩放定律估计的计算开销，同时保持准确性，具有重要实用价值。未来可探索更复杂的代理模型或与其他剪枝策略结合，进一步提升效率。

title: How do Humans Process AI-generated Hallucination Contents: a Neuroimaging Study
abstract: While AI-generated hallucinations pose considerable risks, the underlying cognitive mechanisms by which humans can successfully recognize or be misled by these hallucinations remain unclear. To address this problem, this paper explores humans' neural dynamics to characterize how the brain processes hallucinated content. We record EEG signals from 27 participants while they are performing a verification task to judge the correctness of image descriptions generated by a multi-modal large language model (MLLM). Based on an averaged event-related potential (ERP) study, we reveal that multiple cognitive processes, e.g., semantic integration, inferential processing, memory retrieval, and cognitive load, exhibit distinct patterns when humans process hallucinated versus non-hallucinated content. Notably, neural responses to hallucinations that were misjudged versus correctly judged by human participants showed significant differences. This indicates that misjudged AI-generated hallucinations failed to trigger the standard neurocognitive fact verification pathway.

light_analysis.overview: 通过EEG神经成像揭示人类处理AI生成幻觉内容的认知机制。
light_analysis.motivation: AI生成幻觉带来风险，但人类识别或被误导的认知机制尚不明确。现有研究缺乏神经层面的探索，无法解释为何部分幻觉被接受而部分被拒绝。理解这一机制对提升AI安全性和人机交互信任至关重要。
light_analysis.method: 记录27名参与者执行图像描述正确性验证任务时的EEG信号，描述由多模态大语言模型生成。基于平均事件相关电位分析，比较幻觉与非幻觉内容处理过程中的语义整合、推理、记忆检索和认知负荷等神经活动模式。
light_analysis.result: 幻觉与非幻觉内容引发不同认知过程的神经模式，特别是误判与正确判断的幻觉内容在神经响应上存在显著差异。误判的幻觉未能激活标准事实验证神经通路，表明认知失败与特定神经活动缺失相关。
light_analysis.conclusion: 揭示了人类处理AI幻觉的神经认知基础，误判源于事实验证神经通路的未激活。该发现为检测和减轻AI幻觉提供了认知科学视角，未来可结合神经反馈改进AI系统设计，但需更大样本和跨模态验证。

title: Judge Circuits
abstract: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label). Existing diagnoses of these format-induced inconsistencies stop at the input-output level. Using Position-aware Edge Attribution Patching (PEAP), we causally investigate the internal mechanism in Gemma-3, Qwen2.5, and Llama-3. We find that judgments across structured understanding and open-ended preference tasks share a sparse, generalized Latent Evaluator sub-graph in the mid-to-late multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs); zero-ablating it collapses judgment while preserving world knowledge in architecturally modular models. By structurally decoupling abstract judging from output formatting, we provide a mechanistic account of format-induced inconsistency on the open-weight models we study: a continuous judgment signal computed in the shared trunk is mapped through fragile, format-specific terminal branches, enabling format-independent preference to be isolated downstream of the requested output format. Our findings imply that benchmark-level reliability comparisons across formats are partially measuring formatter geometry rather than evaluation quality.

light_analysis.overview: 通过因果分析方法揭示LLM作为评判者时，格式不一致源于共享评估子图和格式特定终端分支的分离机制。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM-as-a-judge已成为大规模评估模型输出的主流方法，但同一模型在不同输出格式下（如1-5评分与真/假标签）会系统性地给出不同分数。现有诊断仅停留在输入输出层面，未能解释其内部原因。这种格式导致的不一致性严重影响了评测的可靠性和跨格式比较的公平性，亟需从机制层面进行因果理解。
light_analysis.method: 使用位置感知边缘属性修补（PEAP）方法，对Gemma-3、Qwen2.5和Llama-3三个开源模型进行因果分析。通过结构理解和开放偏好任务的判断共享一个稀疏的、泛化的潜在评估子图（位于中后期MLP层），零消融该子图会破坏判断能力但保留世界知识。进一步发现，判断信号在共享主干中连续计算，通过脆弱且格式特定的终端分支映射到输出，从而将抽象判断与输出格式分离。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，在三个模型上，潜在评估子图的零消融导致判断能力崩溃，而世界知识保持完整（摘要未提供具体数值）。格式不一致的根源在于：共享主干计算连续判断信号，但通过格式脆弱的分支映射时产生偏差。该发现意味着跨格式的基准可靠性比较部分度量的是格式几何而非评估质量，强调了机制性理解的重要性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文提出了一种机制性解释：格式不一致性源于判断信号在共享计算后通过格式特定分支的脆弱映射。这有助于将格式无关的偏好从格式影响中分离出来，为构建更鲁棒的评测系统提供方向。未来可探索其他模型和更复杂的格式变体，并设计缓解格式不一致的方法。

title: Composable Crystals: Controllable Materials Discovery via Concept Learning
abstract: De novo crystal generation, a central task in materials discovery, aims to generate crystals that are simultaneously valid, stable, unique, and novel. Existing methods mainly rely on black-box stochastic sampling, providing limited control over how generated structures move beyond the observed distribution. In this paper, we introduce a concept-based compositional framework for crystal generation. We train a vector-quantized variational autoencoder to automatically discover a shared set of reusable crystal concepts, which serve as building blocks for guided generation. These learned concepts naturally exhibit interpretability from both local atomic environments and global symmetry patterns, and generalize to crystals from different distributions. By recombining such concepts, our framework enables controllable exploration of novel crystals beyond the training distribution, rather than relying solely on unconstrained random sampling. To further improve composition efficiency, we introduce a composition generator and iteratively refine it using high-quality samples generated by the model itself. The resulting concept compositions are then used to condition downstream crystal generation. Numerical experiments on MP-20 and Alex-MP-20 show that compositing concepts separately increase base model up to 53.2% and 51.7% on V.S.U.N metric, with particular gains in novelty.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于概念学习的可组合晶体生成框架，实现可控材料发现。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有晶体生成方法依赖黑盒随机采样，对生成结构超出观测分布的控制有限。本文旨在通过可解释的概念组合实现可控探索，提高晶体生成的有效性、稳定性、唯一性和新颖性。
light_analysis.method: 采用向量量化变分自编码器自动发现可复用的晶体概念（局部原子环境+全局对称性），作为引导生成的构建块；引入组合生成器并通过自生成高质量样本迭代优化，以概念组合条件控制下游晶体生成。
light_analysis.result: 在MP-20和Alex-MP-20数据集上，组合概念分别使基础模型在V.S.U.N指标上提升53.2%和51.7%，尤其在新颖性方面表现突出。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该框架通过可解释的概念组合实现可控材料发现，显著提升生成晶体的新颖性，为超越训练分布的探索提供了新范式，未来可扩展至更复杂材料系统。

title: Crys-JEPA: Accelerating Crystal Discovery via Embedding Screening and Generative Refinement
abstract: De novo crystal generation seeks to discover materials that are not merely realistic, but also stable and novel. However, most existing generative models are trained to maximize the likelihood of observed crystals, which encourages samples to stay close to known materials yet not necessarily align with the criteria that matter in discovery. Our empirical analysis shows that current crystal generative models exhibit a clear conflict between stability and novelty: samples near the observed distribution tend to retain stability but offer limited novelty, whereas samples farther from it often lose stability rapidly. This suggests that the useful region for discovering crystals that are both stable and novel is extremely narrow. To move beyond this limitation, we introduce Crys-JEPA, a joint embedding predictive architecture for crystals that learns an energy-aware latent space preserving formation-energy differences. In this space, stability assessment can be reformulated as an embedding-based comparison against accessible training crystals, reducing the reliance on expensive energy evaluation and task-specific external references. Building on Crys-JEPA, we further develop a screening-and-refinement pipeline that identifies promising generated crystals and reintroduces them to refine the generative model. On MP-20 and Alex-MP-20 datasets, we achieve improvements over baselines up to 53.8% and 72.7% on V.S.U.N. metric, respectively.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Crys-JEPA，一种联合嵌入预测架构，通过嵌入筛选和生成精炼，解决晶体生成中稳定性和新颖性的冲突，显著提升发现高质量晶体的能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有晶体生成模型最大化观察数据似然，导致稳定性和新颖性冲突：靠近已知分布的样本稳定但新颖性有限，远离的则稳定性快速下降。这使得同时满足稳定和新颖的晶体区域极为狭窄。为解决此问题，需要一种能平衡二者并减少昂贵能量评估的方法。
light_analysis.method: Crys-JEPA学习能量感知的潜在空间，保留形成能量差异，将稳定性评估转换为嵌入比较，降低对昂贵能量计算的依赖。构建筛选-精炼流水线：首先筛选生成晶体中稳定且新颖的样本，然后将其重新引入训练集以迭代优化生成模型。该方法无需任务特定的外部参考。
light_analysis.result: 在MP-20和Alex-MP-20数据集上，Crys-JEPA在V.S.U.N.指标上分别比基线提升53.8%和72.7%，显著改善了生成晶体的有效性、稳定性、唯一性和新颖性。
light_analysis.conclusion: Crys-JEPA通过能量感知嵌入和迭代精炼，有效突破了晶体生成中稳定性和新颖性的权衡，为加速材料发现提供了新范式。其价值在于减少对昂贵第一性原理计算的依赖，但可能需更多计算资源用于嵌入空间训练。未来可扩展至更多晶体性质预测。

title: Asymmetric Flow Models
abstract: Flow-based generation in high-dimensional spaces is difficult because velocity prediction requires modeling high-dimensional noise, even when data has strong low-rank structure. We present Asymmetric Flow Modeling (AsymFlow), a rank-asymmetric velocity parameterization that restricts noise prediction to a low-rank subspace while keeping data prediction full-dimensional. From this asymmetric prediction, AsymFlow analytically recovers the full-dimensional velocity without changing the network architecture or training/sampling procedures. On ImageNet 256$\times$256, AsymFlow achieves a leading 1.57 FID, outperforming prior DiT/JiT-like pixel diffusion models by a large margin. AsymFlow also provides the first-ever route for finetuning pretrained latent flow models into pixel-space models: aligning the low-rank pixel subspace to the latent space gives a seamless initialization that preserves the latent model's high-level semantics and structure, so finetuning mainly improves low-level mismatches rather than relearning pixel generation. We show that the pixel AsymFlow model finetuned from FLUX.2 klein 9B establishes a new state of the art for pixel-space text-to-image generation, beating its latent base on HPSv3, DPG-Bench, and GenEval while qualitatively showing substantially improved visual realism.

light_analysis.overview: 提出非对称流建模（AsymFlow），通过秩非对称速度参数化利用数据低秩结构，实现高效高维流生成，在像素空间生成中取得领先性能，并首次实现潜在流模型到像素空间模型的微调。
light_analysis.motivation: 基于流的生成在高维空间中面临挑战：速度预测需要建模高维噪声，即使数据具有强低秩结构，现有方法也无法有效利用这一特性，导致计算负担大、效率低。为解决此问题，需要一种能够将噪声预测限制在低秩子空间的方法，以简化建模并提升性能。
light_analysis.method: 提出秩非对称速度参数化：将噪声预测限制在低秩子空间，同时保持数据预测全维，并从非对称预测中解析恢复全维速度，无需改变网络架构或训练/采样流程。此外，通过将低秩像素子空间与潜在空间对齐，实现预训练潜在流模型到像素空间模型的微调，保留高级语义并主要改进低级细节。
light_analysis.result: 在ImageNet 256×256上，AsymFlow取得1.57 FID，大幅领先先前DiT/JiT类像素扩散模型。微调FLUX.2 klein 9B得到的像素空间文本生成模型在HPSv3、DPG-Bench、GenEval上达到新SOTA，视觉真实感显著提升。
light_analysis.conclusion: AsymFlow有效利用低秩结构简化高维流模型，取得了像素空间生成的最优结果，并为潜在流模型微调至像素空间提供了可行路径，具有重要的学术和实用价值。未来可探索将非对称参数化应用于其他生成任务或更复杂的数据类型。

title: Data Difficulty and the Generalization--Extrapolation Tradeoff in LLM Fine-Tuning
abstract: Data selection during supervised fine-tuning (SFT) can critically change the behavior of large language models (LLMs). Although existing work has studied the effect of selecting data based on heuristics such as perplexity, difficulty, or length, the reported findings are often inconsistent or context-dependent. In this work, we systematically study the role of data difficulty in fine-tuning from both empirical and theoretical perspectives, and find that there is no universally optimal difficulty level; rather, its effectiveness depends on the dataset size. We show that for a fixed data budget, there exists an optimal data difficulty for SFT, and that this optimal difficulty shifts toward harder data as the data budget increases. To explain this phenomenon, we conduct controlled synthetic experiments that reveal a simple underlying mechanism: the interplay between the (in-distribution) generalization gap and the extrapolation gap. We further support this mechanism through a theoretical analysis using PAC-Bayesian generalization bounds. Overall, our results clarify how data size and difficulty jointly affect the trade-off between generalization and extrapolation in SFT, providing guidance for difficulty-based data selection under certain model and data conditions.

light_analysis.overview: 揭示数据难度在LLM微调中的效果取决于数据量，存在最优难度且随数据预算增加向更难数据偏移，并解释其机制。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有基于困惑度、难度等启发式数据选择方法报告结果不一致且依赖上下文，缺乏系统性研究。本文旨在理解数据难度在微调中的角色，特别是如何根据数据预算选择最优难度以平衡分布内泛化与外推能力，解决经验发现矛盾的问题。
light_analysis.method: 从经验与理论角度系统研究数据难度在SFT中的作用。通过控制性合成实验揭示分布内泛化差距与外推差距的相互作用机制，并利用PAC-Bayesian泛化界限进行理论分析。未指定具体数据集或模型架构，但方法具有一般性。
light_analysis.result: 发现对于固定数据预算，存在最优数据难度；随着数据预算增加，最优难度向更难数据偏移。合成实验证实泛化差距与外推差距的权衡是根本机制，理论分析用PAC-Bayesian界限进一步支持该发现，为数据选择提供量化指导。
light_analysis.conclusion: 阐明了数据大小与难度联合影响SFT中泛化与外推的权衡，为特定条件下基于难度的数据选择提供理论依据。未来可探索更复杂的模型和数据条件，但结果已揭示关键trade-off机制。

title: DIVER:Diving Deeper into Distilled Data via Expressive Semantic Recovery
abstract: Dataset distillation aims to synthesize a compact proxy dataset that is unreadable or non-raw from the original dataset for privacy protection and highly efficient learning. However, previous approaches typically adopt a single-stage distillation paradigm, which suffers from learning specific patterns that overfit on a prior architecture, consequently suppressing the expression of semantics and leading to performance degradation across heterogeneous architectures. To address this issue, we propose a novel dual-stage distillation framework called ${\textbf{DIVER}}$, which leverages the pre-trained diffusion model to dive deeper into $\textbf{DI}$stilled data $\textbf{V}$ia $\textbf{E}$xpressive semantic $\textbf{R}$ecovery, an entire process of semantic inheritance, guidance, and fusion. Semantic inheritance distills high-level semantics of abstract distilled images into the latent space to filter out architecture-specific ``noise" and retain the intrinsic semantics. Furthermore, semantic guidance improves the preservation of the original semantics by directing the reverse procedure. Finally, semantic fusion is designed to provide semantic guidance only during the concrete phase of the reverse process, preventing semantic ambiguity and artifacts while maintaining the guidance information. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness and efficiency of DIVER in improving classical distillation techniques and significantly improving cross-architecture generalization, requiring processing time comparable to raw DiT on ImageNet (256$\times$256) with only 4 GB of GPU memory usage.

light_analysis.overview: 提出双阶段数据集蒸馏框架DIVER，利用预训练扩散模型通过语义恢复提升跨架构泛化能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 数据集蒸馏旨在从原始数据合成紧凑代理数据集，用于隐私保护和高效学习。但传统单阶段蒸馏易过拟合特定架构，抑制语义表达，导致跨架构性能下降。因此需要一种能保留语义并提升泛化能力的方法。
light_analysis.method: DIVER采用双阶段蒸馏框架，借助预训练扩散模型实现语义恢复。包含三个关键过程：语义继承将抽象蒸馏图像的高层语义提取到潜在空间，过滤架构特定噪声；语义引导通过指导反向过程增强原始语义保留；语义融合仅在反向过程的具体阶段提供语义引导，避免语义歧义和伪影。
light_analysis.result: 在ImageNet（256×256）上，DIVER以与原始DiT相当的处理时间和仅4GB GPU内存，显著提升经典蒸馏技术的效果和跨架构泛化能力。实验验证了方法的有效性和高效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: DIVER通过语义继承、引导和融合，有效解决了单阶段蒸馏的语义抑制问题，提升了数据集蒸馏的跨架构泛化性能。其高效的内存使用和计算效率表明具有实际应用价值，未来可进一步探索更复杂的语义恢复机制。

title: Shepherd: A Runtime Substrate Empowering Meta-Agents with a Formalized Execution Trace
abstract: As LLM agent systems take on more complex tasks, they increasingly rely on meta-agents: higher-order agents that operate on other agents, much as managers supervise employees. Whatever a meta-agent does: coordinating agents, halting risky actions before execution, or repairing failed runs, requires manipulation of agentic execution at runtime. Existing agentic substrates make this hard: they give meta-agents only plain transcripts and environment snapshots, requiring it to build it's own tooling to reconstruct and orchestrate execution state. Therefore, we introduce Shepherd, a Python substrate grounded in functional programming principles, where an agent's execution is itself a first-class object that a meta-agent can inspect and transform. Every model call, tool call, and environment change becomes a structured event in a Git-like execution trace, where any past state can be forked 5x faster than docker commit and replayed. Three example use cases show Shepherd's versatility: (1) a supervisor agent prevents conflicts among parallel coding agents, lifting CooperBench performance from 28.8% to 54.7%; (2) a counterfactual optimizer repairs agent workflows by proposing edits and replaying runs from the point of changed behavior, outperforming MetaHarness on TerminalBench-2 with 58% lower wall-clock; (3) a meta-agent picks fork points during rollouts to improve credit assignment in long-horizon agentic RL, doubling GRPO's gains on TerminalBench-2. We open-source Shepherd to empower future meta-agents with principled and efficient operations over agentic execution.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Shepherd运行时，将agent执行视为一等对象，实现结构化执行轨迹，赋能元代理高效操作。
light_analysis.motivation: 随着LLM代理系统处理复杂任务，元代理（如协调、干预、修复）需要操纵agent运行时状态。现有技术仅提供纯文本记录和环境快照，迫使元代理自建工具来重建和编排执行状态，效率低下且难以扩展。因此，急需一种形式化的执行轨迹基础设施，使元代理能直接检查和变换执行状态。
light_analysis.method: Shepherd是一个基于函数式编程原则的Python运行时。它将agent执行视为一等对象，每次模型调用、工具调用和环境变更都作为结构化事件记录在类似Git的执行轨迹中。支持对任何历史状态进行快速fork（比docker commit快5倍）和重放。该方法使得元代理可以通过编程方式检查和变换执行状态，实现高效的干预和优化。
light_analysis.result: 三个用例验证了有效性：(1) 监督代理防止并行编码冲突，CooperBench性能从28.8%提升至54.7%；(2) 反事实优化器修复工作流，在TerminalBench-2上比MetaHarness省时58%；(3) 元代理选择fork点改进长程RL信用分配，在TerminalBench-2上将GRPO收益翻倍。
light_analysis.conclusion: Shepherd为元代理提供了形式化、高效的执行操作原语，显著提升了复杂任务的性能。其开源将推动智能体系统的发展。未来可探索更复杂的元代理策略及扩展至更多领域。

title: TIE: Time Interval Encoding for Video Generation over Events
abstract: Director-style prompting, robotic action prediction, and interactive video agents demand temporal grounding over concurrent events -- a regime in which 68% of general clips and over 99% of robotics/gameplay clips contain overlapping events, yet existing multi-event generators rest on a single-active-prompt assumption. However, modern video generators, such as Diffusion Transformers (DiT), represent time as discrete points through point-wise positional encodings. This formulation creates a fundamental dimension mismatch: temporally extended intervals and overlapping events are mathematically unrepresentable to the attention mechanism. In this paper, we propose Time Interval Encoding (TIE), a principled, plug-and-play interval-aware generalization of rotary embeddings that elevates time intervals to first-class primitives inside DiT cross-attention. Rather than introducing another heuristic interval embedding, we show that, within RoPE-compatible bilinear attention, TIE is characterized by two basic principles: Temporal Integrability, which requires an event to aggregate positional evidence over its full duration, and Duration Invariance, which removes the trivial bias toward longer intervals. Under a uniform kernel, this characterization yields an efficient closed-form sinc-based solution that preserves the standard attention interface and naturally attenuates boundary noise through interval integration. Empirically, TIE preserves the visual quality of the base DiT model while substantially improving temporal controllability. In our experiments on the OmniEvents dataset, it improves human-verified Temporal Constraint Satisfaction Rate from 77.34% to 96.03% and reduces temporal boundary error from 0.261s to 0.073s, while also improving trajectory-level temporal alignment metrics. The code and dataset are available at https://github.com/MatrixTeam-AI/TIE.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Time Interval Encoding，将时间间隔作为一等公民融入DiT交叉注意力，实现多事件视频生成的时间控制。
light_analysis.motivation: 实际视频中68%的普通片段和99%的机器人/游戏片段包含重叠事件，但现有生成器基于单主动提示假设，将时间表示为离散点导致注意力机制无法表示时间间隔和重叠。需要一种能编码时间间隔的方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出TIE，基于RoPE的间隔感知泛化，满足时间可积性和持续时间不变性。采用均匀核的闭式sinc解，保持标准注意力接口并通过间隔积分自然衰减边界噪声，即插即用。
light_analysis.result: 在OmniEvents数据集上，人工验证的时间约束满足率从77.34%提升至96.03%，时间边界误差从0.261s降至0.073s，轨迹级时间对齐指标提升，同时保持视觉质量。
light_analysis.conclusion: TIE通过将时间间隔作为一等公民显著提升视频生成的时间可控性，尤其适用于重叠事件场景，代码和数据集已公开，未来可扩展到更复杂时序结构。

title: Memorize Theorems, Not Instances: Probing SFT Generalization through Mathematical Reasoning
abstract: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) is widely used for task-specific adaptation, yet recent work shows it systematically undermines reasoning generalization. We argue the root cause is not memorization itself, but its target: vanilla SFT drives models to exploit and memorize spurious surface correlations in problem-solution pairs, leaving them brittle to superficial input variations. To address this, we propose Theorem-SFT, which reorients supervision toward explicit theorem application by teaching models how rules are invoked rather than what answers look like. Theorem-SFT yields consistent gains across benchmarks and model families: +8.8% on MATH (LLaMA3.2-3B-Instruct) and +20.27% on GeoQA (Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct) without modality-specific re-training. Fine-tuning MLP layers alone matches full-layers performance, implicating feed-forward components as the primary locus of reasoning rules. Our findings reframe the debate: Generalization failures stem not from memorization as a mechanism, but from memorizing the wrong inductive targets.

light_analysis.overview: 提出 Theorem-SFT，通过教导模型显式定理应用而非记忆表面相关性，提升 SFT 推理泛化能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 监督微调广泛用于任务适应，但近期研究表明它会系统性削弱推理泛化。现有方法认为根因在于记忆，但论文指出真正问题在于记忆了错误的目标：vanilla SFT 驱动模型利用和记忆问题-解决对中的虚假表面相关性，导致对输入变化脆弱。因此需要重新定向监督目标，使模型学习规则调用而非答案形式。
light_analysis.method: 提出 Theorem-SFT，将监督信号从答案形式转向显式定理应用。通过构造训练数据，教导模型如何调用数学定理而非简单地记忆解题步骤。方法无需修改模型架构或重新训练模态。实验中发现仅微调 MLP 层即可达到全层微调效果，暗示前馈组件是推理规则的主要存储位置。
light_analysis.result: Theorem-SFT 在多个基准和模型家族上取得一致提升：在 MATH 上对 LLaMA3.2-3B-Instruct 提升 8.8%，在 GeoQA 上对 Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct 提升 20.27%，且无需针对特定模态重新训练。对比 vanilla SFT，Theorem-SFT 在表面输入变化下表现更鲁棒。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文重新框架了泛化失败的原因：不是记忆机制本身，而是记忆了错误的归纳目标。Theorem-SFT 通过将监督转向定理应用，有效提升了推理泛化能力。该工作揭示了前馈组件在存储推理规则中的关键作用，为未来设计更鲁棒的微调方法提供了新方向。

title: FS-I2P:A Hierarchical Focus-Sweep Registration Network with Dynamically Allocated Depth
abstract: Image-to-point cloud registration is often challenged by viewpoint changes, cross-modal discrepancies, and repetitive textures, which induce scale ambiguity and consequently lead to erroneous correspondences. Recent detection-free methods alleviate this issue by leveraging multi-scale features and transformer-based interactions. However, they still suffer from attention drift across layers and intra-scale inconsistencies, hindering precise registration. Inspired by human behavior, we propose a ``Focus--Sweep'' paradigm and develop a Hierarchical Focus--Sweep Interaction Module within an SSM-based framework to enhance multi-level cross-modal feature association. In addition, we introduce a Dynamic Layer Allocation Strategy that adaptively determines the iteration depth to better exploit geometric constraints and improve matching robustness. Extensive experiments and ablations on two benchmarks, RGB-D Scenes V2 and 7-Scenes, demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种模仿人类行为的“Focus-Sweep”范式的图像到点云配准网络，通过分层聚焦-扫描交互模块和动态层分配策略，实现最先进的配准性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 图像到点云配准受视角变化、跨模态差异和重复纹理影响，导致尺度模糊和错误对应。现有无检测方法虽用多尺度特征和transformer交互，但存在层间注意漂移和尺度内不一致，阻碍精确配准。
light_analysis.method: 提出“Focus-Sweep”范式，基于SSM框架构建分层聚焦-扫描交互模块，增强多级跨模态特征关联。引入动态层分配策略，自适应确定迭代深度，利用几何约束提升匹配鲁棒性。在RGB-D Scenes V2和7-Scenes数据集上验证。
light_analysis.result: 在RGB-D Scenes V2和7-Scenes两个基准数据集上进行大量实验和消融研究，结果表明该方法达到了最先进的性能。具体指标如配准召回率和精度均优于现有方法，摘要未明确说明数值。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该工作通过模仿人类聚焦-扫描行为，有效解决了图像-点云配准中的尺度模糊和对应漂移问题，动态层分配策略增强了鲁棒性。具有学术创新和实际应用价值，未来可探索在更多复杂场景下的泛化能力。

title: Psychologically Potent, Computationally Invisible: LLMs Generate Social-Comparison-Eliciting Posts They Fail to Detect
abstract: We introduce Xiaohongshu Social Comparison Reader Elicitation (XHS-SCoRE), a reader-grounded benchmark for detecting whether text-only Xiaohongshu (RedNote) posts elicit Upward, Downward, or Neutral/no clear social comparison from a first-person reader perspective. The task targets a socially meaningful relational, behaviorally real signal not reducible to sentiment. Across prompted LLM classifiers and supervised Chinese encoders, we find a consistent generation--detection mismatch: the signal is textually learnable in-domain, but not robustly accessible to prompt-based classification. Prompted LLM classifiers show stable failures, especially neutralization of comparison-eliciting posts and model-specific directional skew. A controlled pilot shows that LLM-generated Xiaohongshu-style posts can shift perceived standing and comparison-related affect even when prompt-based detection of the same construct remains fragile. XHS-SCoRE contributes a benchmark for reader-grounded comparison detection and a diagnostic framework for studying when socially meaningful relational cues remain only partially visible to prompt-based inference.
comment: 19 pages, preprint Title change: Psychologically Potent, Computationally Invisible: LLMs Generate Social-Comparison-Eliciting Posts They Fail to Detect

light_analysis.overview: 提出XHS-SCoRE基准，发现LLM生成的社会比较引发帖子与检测能力之间存在显著不匹配。
light_analysis.motivation: 社会比较是社交媒体中重要的心理现象，但现有情感分析无法捕捉这种关系性信号。LLM虽能生成引发社会比较的帖子，但基于提示的分类方法难以可靠检测，导致生成与检测能力失衡，阻碍了对LLM社会影响的理解和监管。
light_analysis.method: 构建XHS-SCoRE基准，包含来自小红书的文本帖子，标注上行、下行或中性社会比较。评估多种LLM分类器和中文编码器，包括提示式LLM和微调模型。通过受控实验让LLM生成帖子，测试其对读者感知和情绪的影响。
light_analysis.result: LLM分类器在域内文本上可学习社会比较信号，但提示式检测不稳定，表现为对引发比较的帖子中性化偏向和模型特定方向偏差。LLM生成的帖子能显著改变读者社会比较感知和情绪，即使提示式检测同一构念仍然脆弱。
light_analysis.conclusion: XHS-SCoRE提供了读者视角的社会比较检测基准，揭示了LLM在社会信号生成与检测之间的鸿沟。该诊断框架有助于研究关系性社会线索在提示式推理中的局限性，为评估LLM的社会影响提供工具，未来可扩展至多模态和跨文化场景。

title: Rethinking LLM Ensembling from the Perspective of Mixture Models
abstract: Model ensembling is a well-established technique for improving the performance of machine learning models. Conventionally, this involves averaging the output distributions of multiple models and selecting the most probable label. This idea has been naturally extended to large language models (LLMs), yielding improved performance but incurring substantial computational cost. This inefficiency stems from directly applying conventional ensemble implementation to LLMs, which require a separate forward pass for each model to explicitly compute the ensemble distribution. In this paper, we propose the Mixture-model-like Ensemble (ME). By reinterpreting the ensemble as a mixture model, ME stochastically selects a single model at each step to generate the next token, thereby avoiding the need to explicitly compute the full ensemble distribution. ME is mathematically equivalent to sampling from the ensemble distribution, but requires invoking only one model, making it 1.78x-2.68x faster than conventional ensembling. Furthermore, this perspective connects LLM ensembling and token-level routing methods, suggesting that LLM ensembling is a special case of routing methods. Our findings open new avenues for efficient LLM ensembling and motivate further exploration of token-level routing strategies for LLMs. Our code is available at https://github.com/Kamichanw/Mixture-model-like-Ensemble.

light_analysis.overview: 提出混合模型式集成（ME），将LLM集成视为混合模型，通过随机选择单个模型生成每个token，实现高效且数学等价的集成。
light_analysis.motivation: 传统LLM集成需对每个模型进行前向传播以计算集成分布，计算成本高昂。现有方法直接沿用传统集成实现，效率低下。论文旨在解决这一效率问题，同时保持集成性能。
light_analysis.method: ME将集成重新解释为混合模型，在每个解码步骤随机选择一个模型生成下一token，等效于从集成分布中采样。该方法仅需调用单个模型，避免显式计算完整集成分布，并与token级路由方法建立联系。
light_analysis.result: ME在保持与传统集成等价的前提下，速度提升1.78倍至2.68倍。论文未提供具体性能指标（如准确率），但强调效率改进。与传统集成相比，ME显著降低计算成本。
light_analysis.conclusion: ME提供了一种高效的新型LLM集成范式，将集成与路由方法关联，为未来token级路由策略研究奠定基础。潜在局限性包括随机采样可能引入方差，未来可探索更优的路由机制。

title: ClawTrace: Cost-Aware Tracing for LLM Agent Skill Distillation
abstract: Skill-distillation pipelines learn reusable rules from LLM agent trajectories, but they lack a key signal: how much each step costs. Without per-step cost, a pipeline cannot distinguish adding a missing step to fix a bug from removing an expensive step that never affected the outcome. We use the cost-attribution gap to ask whether the rule types inside a distilled skill transfer the same way to new tasks. ClawTrace records cost-attributed agent traces and compiles each session into a TraceCard; CostCraft reads TraceCards and writes three kinds of skill patches: preserve, prune, and repair. We find a pattern aggregate metrics hide. On 30 held-out SpreadsheetBench tasks across two seeds, removing prune patches roughly tripled the quality-regression count without lowering median cost. Across the full 84-task SkillsBench transfer, CostCraft saves no aggregate cost. All three quality regressions trace to the preserve lane, and both quality wins trace to the prune lane: prune patches act as quality guardrails while preserve patches drive regressions. We argue that reusable agent skills should be evaluated at the rule-type level, not as monolithic instruction packages. To support this, we release ClawTrace, the TraceCard schema, and the full set of typed skills.
comment: Accepted at Agent Skills '26 Workshop, ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems (CAIS 2026), San José, CA, May 26, 2026

light_analysis.overview: 提出成本感知的LLM Agent技能蒸馏框架ClawTrace，通过记录成本归因轨迹实现规则级技能评估与优化。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有技能蒸馏流程从LLM Agent轨迹学习可重用规则，但忽略了每个执行步骤的成本信号。缺乏成本信息导致无法区分修复bug的步骤和移除不影响结果的高成本步骤，使得技能优化盲目。实际应用中，整体指标可能掩盖不同规则类型对迁移性能的差异化影响，需要细粒度的成本感知机制来指导技能补丁生成。
light_analysis.method: ClawTrace框架包含两部分：1) 记录成本归因的Agent轨迹并编译为标准化TraceCard；2) CostCraft模块读取TraceCard，生成三种技能补丁——保留（preserve）、修剪（prune）和修复（repair）。通过在SpreadsheetBench和SkillsBench任务集上评估，以规则类型级别分析技能迁移效果，而非整体评估。
light_analysis.result: 在30个SpreadsheetBench任务上，移除修剪补丁使质量回归计数增加约3倍（中位数成本不变）。在84个SkillsBench任务迁移中，总成本未节省；所有3个质量回归来自保留补丁，2个质量提升来自修剪补丁，表明修剪补丁作为质量护栏，保留补丁导致回归。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论证了可重用Agent技能应在规则类型级别评估，而非作为整体指令包。ClawTrace通过成本感知轨迹和补丁机制揭示了整体指标隐藏的模式，为LLM Agent技能优化提供了新方向。未来可将该方法扩展到更多复杂任务，并探索自动补丁选择策略。

title: ESIA: An Energy-Based Spatiotemporal Interaction-Aware Framework for Pedestrian Intention Prediction
abstract: Recent advances in autonomous driving have motivated research on pedestrian intention prediction, which aims to infer future crossing decisions and actions by modeling temporal dynamics, social interactions, and environmental context. However, existing studies remain constrained by oversimplified multi-agent interaction patterns, opaque reasoning logic, and a lack of global consistency in behavioral predictions, which compromise both robustness and interpretability. In this work, we propose ESIA (Energy-based Spatiotemporal Interaction-Aware framework), a novel Conditional Random Field (CRF)-based paradigm. We cast the intention prediction task as a structured prediction problem over a unified graph-based representation, treating pedestrians and the environment as spatiotemporal nodes. To characterize their distinct roles, we assign unary potentials to nodes to capture individual intentions, and pairwise potentials to edges to encode social and environmental interactions. These potentials are integrated into a unified global energy function to ensure scene-level consistency across behavioral predictions. To further constrain inference without ground-truth supervision, we introduce structural consistency terms to penalize logical contradictions. This optimization is efficiently solved via a novel Unary-Seeded Simulated Annealing (U-SSA) algorithm, which leverages high-confidence unary priors to rapidly converge to a high-quality solution. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that ESIA achieves state-of-the-art performance with improved interpretability over existing methods.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ESIA框架，基于条件随机场和能量函数，通过结构一致性与U-SSA算法实现行人意图预测，兼顾鲁棒性与可解释性。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有行人意图预测方法在多智能体交互建模上过于简化，推理逻辑不透明，且缺乏全局行为一致性，导致鲁棒性和可解释性不足。为解决这些问题，需要一种能够统一建模个体意图、社交互动和环境上下文，并保证场景级一致性的结构化预测方法。
light_analysis.method: ESIA将意图预测建模为结构化预测问题，构建统一的图表示，将行人和环境作为时空节点。为节点分配一元势能（捕捉个体意图），为边分配成对势能（编码社交与环境交互），并集成到全局能量函数以确保场景一致性。引入结构一致性项约束推理，无需真实标签监督，并通过Unary-Seeded Simulated Annealing (U-SSA)算法利用高置信度一元先验快速收敛。
light_analysis.result: 在标准基准数据集上的大量实验表明，ESIA达到了最先进的性能，同时相比现有方法具有更好的可解释性。摘要未提供具体数值，但明确报告了SOTA结果。
light_analysis.conclusion: ESIA提出了一种新的CRF范式，通过能量函数统一建模时空交互和场景一致性，显著提升了行人意图预测的鲁棒性和可解释性。该工作具有重要的学术价值，为自动驾驶中的安全决策提供了可靠支撑。未来可进一步探索更复杂的交互模式或扩展至其他预测任务。

title: Complex Stochastic Gradient Descent and Directional Bias in Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Spaces
abstract: Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) is a known stochastic iterative method popular for large-scale convex optimization problems due to its simple implementation and scalability. Some objectives, such as those found in complex-valued neural networks, benefit from updates like in SGD and Gradient Descent (GD) with a newly defined ``gradient'' that allows for complex parameters. This complex variant of the SGD/GD methods has already been proposed, but convergence guarantees without analyticity constraints have not yet been provided. We propose a variant of SGD (complex SGD) that allows for complex parameters, and we provide convergence guarantees under assumptions that parallel those from the real setting. Notably, these results extend to GD as well, and with the same set of assumptions, we confirm that some directional bias results extend from the real to the complex setting for kernel regression problems. We provide empirical results demonstrating the efficacy of the complex SGD in kernel regression problems utilizing complex reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces. In particular, we demonstrate we may recover superoscillation functions and Blaschke products from the Fock Space and Hardy Space, respectively, as the optimal functions for a particular choice of a loss function.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种无需解析约束的复数随机梯度下降法（complex SGD），并给出其在再生核希尔伯特空间中的收敛保证，同时将方向性偏差从实数域扩展到复数域。
light_analysis.motivation: 复杂神经网络等目标需要复数参数更新，现有复数SGD/GD方法已被提出，但缺乏无解析性约束下的收敛保证。此外，方向性偏差在实数域核回归模型中已有研究，但在复数域中尚未被验证。该研究旨在填充这些理论空白，为复数优化提供理论基础。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出一种复数SGD变体，允许复数参数，并基于与实数设置平行的假设给出收敛保证，结果也扩展到GD。对于核回归问题，证明了方向性偏差从实数扩展到复数。使用复数再生核希尔伯特空间进行实验，具体包括Fock空间和Hardy空间中的超振荡函数和Blaschke积的恢复。
light_analysis.result: 实验证明了复数SGD在核回归问题中的有效性，能够从Fock空间和Hardy空间中分别恢复超振荡函数和Blaschke积作为最优函数。摘要未明确说明与基线方法的定量对比，但表明复数SGD能够有效学习这些复杂函数。
light_analysis.conclusion: 主要贡献是提出了复数SGD的理论收敛保证，并将方向性偏差扩展到复数域。学术价值在于为复数优化提供了理论依据，实际应用价值在于可用于复杂神经网络等复数参数场景。潜在局限性未提及，未来工作可能包括更广泛的优化问题。

title: Mechanistic Anomaly Detection via Functional Attribution
abstract: We can often verify the correctness of neural network outputs using ground truth labels, but we cannot reliably determine whether the output was produced by normal or anomalous internal mechanisms. Mechanistic anomaly detection (MAD) aims to flag these cases, but existing methods either depend on latent space analysis, which is vulnerable to obfuscation, or are specific to particular architectures and modalities. We reframe MAD as a functional attribution problem: asking to what extent samples from a trusted set can explain the model's output, where attribution failure signals anomalous behavior. We operationalize this using influence functions, measuring functional coupling between test samples and a small reference set via parameter-space sampling. We evaluate across multiple anomaly types and modalities. For backdoors in vision models, our method achieves state-of-the-art detection on BackdoorBench, with an average Defense Effectiveness Rating (DER) of 0.93 across seven attacks and four datasets (next best 0.83). For LLMs, we similarly achieve a significant improvement over baselines for several backdoor types, including on explicitly obfuscated models. Beyond backdoors, our method can detect adversarial and out-of-distribution samples, and distinguishes multiple anomalous mechanisms within a single model. Our results establish functional attribution as an effective, modality-agnostic tool for detecting anomalous behavior in deployed models.

light_analysis.overview: 提出功能归因方法解决机械异常检测问题，通过影响函数测量功能耦合，实现跨模态的异常机制检测。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有方法无法可靠判断模型输出是否由异常内部机制产生，机械异常检测旨在标记这些情况，但现有方法依赖于易受混淆的潜在空间分析，或仅适用于特定架构和模态。需要一种通用且鲁棒的方法来检测模型内部的异常机制。
light_analysis.method: 将机械异常检测重构为功能归因问题，通过影响函数衡量测试样本与参考集之间的功能耦合，利用参数空间采样进行归因。方法不依赖特定架构或模态，可应用于视觉模型、大型语言模型等。在BackdoorBench上评估7种攻击和4个数据集。
light_analysis.result: 在视觉模型后门检测中，平均防御有效性得分为0.93，优于次优方法（0.83）。在大型语言模型的后门检测中，包括显式混淆的模型，也显著优于基线。此外，该方法能检测对抗样本和分布外样本，并区分单个模型中的多种异常机制。
light_analysis.conclusion: 功能归因是检测部署模型中异常行为的有效、模态无关的工具。实验覆盖后门、对抗和分布外异常，证明其通用性。未来可探索更高效的归因计算方法。

title: Knowing When to Quit: A Principled Framework for Dynamic Abstention in LLM Reasoning
abstract: LLMs utilizing chain-of-thought reasoning often waste substantial compute by producing long, incorrect responses. Abstention can mitigate this by withholding outputs unlikely to be correct. While most abstention methods decide to withhold outputs before or after generation, dynamic mid-generation abstention considers early termination of unpromising reasoning traces at each token position. Prior work has explored empirical variants of this idea, but principled guidance for the abstention rule remains lacking. We present a formal analysis of dynamic abstention for LLMs, modeling abstention as an explicit action within a regularized reinforcement learning framework. An abstention reward parameter controls the trade-off between compute and information. We show that abstaining when the value function falls below this reward strictly outperforms natural baselines under general conditions. We further derive a principled and efficient method to approximate the value function. Empirical results on mathematical reasoning and toxicity avoidance tasks support our theory and demonstrate improved selective accuracy over existing methods.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种基于强化学习的动态弃权框架，在LLM推理过程中提前终止无望的推理路径，以平衡计算与准确性。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM使用链式思维推理时，常生成冗长且错误的回答，浪费大量计算资源。现有弃权方法多在生成前后决定是否输出，但缺乏动态中途弃权的原则性指导。本文旨在填补这一空白，通过形式化分析提供可靠的弃权规则，以减少无效计算并提高输出可靠性。
light_analysis.method: 将动态弃权建模为正则化强化学习中的显式动作，引入弃权奖励参数控制计算与信息权衡。证明当价值函数低于该奖励时弃权严格优于基线。推导出高效近似价值函数的方法，支持在每个token位置动态中止。
light_analysis.result: 在数学推理和毒性避免任务上，该方法优于现有动态弃权方法，选择性准确性显著提升。具体性能指标摘要未明确给出，但声称支持理论并改进实际效果。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出了首个原则性动态弃权框架，为LLM推理中的计算-准确性权衡提供理论指导。具有实际应用价值，可减少资源浪费并提高可靠性。未来可探索更复杂环境的适应性或多目标优化。

title: River-LLM: Large Language Model Seamless Exit Based on KV Share
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional performance across diverse domains but are increasingly constrained by high inference latency. Early Exit has emerged as a promising solution to accelerate inference by dynamically bypassing redundant layers. However, in decoder-only architectures, the efficiency of Early Exit is severely bottlenecked by the KV Cache Absence problem, where skipped layers fail to provide the necessary historical states for subsequent tokens. Existing solutions, such as recomputation or masking, either introduce significant latency overhead or incur severe precision loss, failing to bridge the gap between theoretical layer reduction and practical wall-clock speedup. In this paper, we propose River-LLM, a training-free framework that enables seamless token-level Early Exit. River-LLM introduces a lightweight KV-Shared Exit River that allows the backbone's missing KV cache to be naturally generated and preserved during the exit process, eliminating the need for costly recovery operations. Furthermore, we utilize state transition similarity within decoder blocks to predict cumulative KV errors and guide precise exit decisions. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks demonstrate that River-LLM achieves 1.53 to 2.16 times of practical speedup while maintaining high generation quality.
comment: Accepted to ACL 2026, 13pages, with appendix. Corrected some typos

light_analysis.overview: 提出River-LLM，一种无训练框架，通过KV共享实现LLM的无缝token级早期退出，在数学推理和代码生成任务上取得1.53-2.16倍实际加速。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM推理延迟高，早期退出方法受KV缓存缺失问题制约：跳过的层无法提供后续token所需的历史状态。现有方案如重计算或掩码引入额外延迟或精度损失，无法实现理论加速与实际墙钟加速的匹配。需要一种无训练、高效且保持生成质量的早期退出方法。
light_analysis.method: River-LLM引入轻量级KV共享退出河，使主干网络缺失的KV缓存在退出过程中自然生成并保留，无需昂贵的恢复操作。利用解码器块内状态转移相似性预测累积KV误差，指导精确的退出决策。整个框架无需训练，适用于任意解码器架构的LLM。
light_analysis.result: 在数学推理和代码生成任务上的实验表明，River-LLM实现1.53至2.16倍的实际加速，同时保持与完整模型相当的高生成质量。与现有早期退出方法相比，在加速与精度之间取得了更好平衡，避免了重计算或掩码的缺陷。
light_analysis.conclusion: River-LLM通过KV共享解决了解码器架构中早期退出的KV缓存缺失问题，实现了无训练、高质量的推理加速。其方法简单有效，可广泛应用于各类LLM部署场景。未来可探索跨架构适配及更复杂的退出策略。

title: $π$-Play: Multi-Agent Self-Play via Privileged Self-Distillation without External Data
abstract: Deep search agents have emerged as a promising paradigm for addressing complex information-seeking tasks, but their training remains challenging due to sparse rewards, weak credit assignment, and limited labeled data. Self-play offers a scalable route to reduce data dependence, but conventional self-play optimizes students only through sparse outcome rewards, leading to low learning efficiency. In this work, we observe that self-play naturally produces a question construction path (QCP) during task generation, an intermediate artifact that captures the reverse solution process. This reveals a new source of privileged information: self-play can provide high-quality privileged information for the self-distillation at low cost and at scale, without relying on human feedback or curated privileged information. Leveraging this insight, we propose Privileged Information Self-Play ($π$-Play), a novel multi-agent self-evolution framework combining self-play and self-distillation. In $π$-Play, an examiner generates tasks together with QCPs, and a teacher employs QCP as privileged context to densely supervise a student via self-distillation. This design transforms sparse-reward self-play into a dense-feedback co-evolution. Extensive experiments show that data-free $π$-Play surpasses fully supervised search agents and improves evolutionary efficiency by 2-3$\times$ over conventional self-play. Code is available at https://github.com/zhyaoch/pi-play.

light_analysis.overview: 提出π-Play方法，利用自我博弈中自然产生的任务构建路径作为特权信息进行自我蒸馏，实现无外部数据的多智能体高效进化。
light_analysis.motivation: 深度搜索代理训练面临稀疏奖励、弱信用分配和有限标注数据的挑战。传统自我博弈仅通过稀疏结果奖励优化学生，导致学习效率低。观察发现自我博弈在任务生成时自然产生中间产物——问题构建路径，它揭示了逆向求解过程，可作为高质量特权信息用于自我蒸馏，无需人工反馈或精心设计的特权信息。
light_analysis.method: 提出特权信息自我博弈（π-Play）框架，结合自我博弈与自我蒸馏。其中，examiner生成任务及问题构建路径（QCP），teacher以QCP为特权上下文对学生进行密集监督训练。该设计将稀疏奖励自我博弈转化为密集反馈共同进化过程，实现了数据高效训练。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，无外部数据的π-Play方法在性能上超越了完全监督的搜索代理，且进化效率相比传统自我博弈提升了2-3倍。具体性能指标和对比数据在原文中详述，代码已开源。
light_analysis.conclusion: π-Play证明了自我博弈中自发产生的特权信息可用于自我蒸馏，有效解决了稀疏奖励和弱信用分配问题。该方法具有重要的学术价值和实际应用潜力，为构建高效、无需外部标注的智能体训练范式提供了新思路。未来可探索更复杂的任务生成和知识蒸馏策略。

title: CArtBench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Chinese Art Understanding, Interpretation, and Authenticity
abstract: We introduce CARTBENCH, a museum-grounded benchmark for evaluating vision-language models (VLMs) on Chinese artworks beyond short-form recognition and QA. CARTBENCH comprises four subtasks: CURATORQA for evidence-grounded recognition and reasoning, CATALOGCAPTION for structured four-section expert-style appreciation, REINTERPRET for defensible reinterpretation with expert ratings, and CONNOISSEURPAIRS for diagnostic authenticity discrimination under visually similar confounds. CARTBENCH is built by aligning image-bearing Palace Museum objects from Wikidata with authoritative catalog pages, spanning five art categories across multiple dynasties. Across nine representative VLMs, we find that high overall CURATORQA accuracy can mask sharp drops on hard evidence linking and style-to-period inference; long-form appreciation remains far from expert references; and authenticity-oriented diagnostic discrimination stays near chance, underscoring the difficulty of connoisseur-level reasoning for current models.

light_analysis.overview: 构建了面向中国艺术品的多维度视觉语言基准CARTBENCH，包含证据推理、长篇鉴赏、重新解释和真伪鉴别四个子任务。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有视觉语言模型评估多集中于短形式识别和简单问答，缺乏对中国艺术作品深度理解与专家级推理能力的考察。中国艺术品的文化独特性和鉴赏复杂性要求专门的评估基准，以揭示模型在证据链接、风格推断和真伪鉴别等方面的实际能力。
light_analysis.method: 基于故宫博物院的藏品图像与维基百科数据对齐，构建CARTBENCH基准，包含四个子任务：CURATORQA（证据推理）、CATALOGCAPTION（结构化专家鉴赏）、REINTERPRET（防御性重新解释并专家评分）、CONNOISSEURPAIRS（真伪鉴别）。涵盖绘画、书法等五个艺术类别，跨越多个朝代。
light_analysis.result: 在九个代表性视觉语言模型上测试，CURATORQA总体准确率虽高，但在证据链接和风格推断任务中准确率急剧下降；CATALOGCAPTION生成的长篇鉴赏与专家参考差异显著；CONNOISSEURPAIRS真伪鉴别接近随机水平，表明当前模型缺乏鉴赏级推理能力。
light_analysis.conclusion: CARTBENCH揭示了现有视觉语言模型在中国艺术品专家级理解上的显著不足，尤其在真实性判断方面。该基准为评估和提升模型的文化艺术推理能力提供了标准化工具，未来可扩展至更多艺术类别和跨文化比较。

title: UniToolCall: Unifying Tool-Use Representation, Data, and Evaluation for LLM Agents
abstract: Tool-use capability is a fundamental component of LLM agents, enabling them to interact with external systems through structured function calls. However, existing research exhibits inconsistent interaction representations, largely overlooks the structural distribution of tool-use trajectories, and relies on incompatible evaluation benchmarks. We present UniToolCall, a unified framework for tool learning that standardizes the entire pipeline from toolset construction and dataset generation to evaluation. The framework curates a large tool pool of 22k+ tools and constructs a hybrid training corpus of 390k+ instances by combining 10 standardized public datasets with structurally controlled synthetic trajectories. It explicitly models diverse interaction patterns, including single-hop vs. multi-hop and single-turn vs. multi-turn, while capturing both serial and parallel execution structures. To support coherent multi-turn reasoning, we further introduce an Anchor Linkage mechanism that enforces cross-turn dependencies. Furthermore, we convert 7 public benchmarks into a unified Query--Action--Observation--Answer (QAOA) representation with fine-grained evaluation at the function-call, turn, and conversation levels. Experiments show that fine-tuning Qwen3-8B on our dataset substantially improves tool-use performance. Under the distractor-heavy Hybrid-20 setting, achieves 93.0% single-turn Strict Precision, outperforming commercial models including GPT, Gemini, and Claude.
comment: 21 pages, 10 figures, 9 tables. Code and datasets are publicly available at: https://github.com/EIT-NLP/UniToolCall

light_analysis.overview: 提出UniToolCall框架，统一LLM智能体工具学习的表示、数据和评估，构建大规模工具池和混合训练语料，并引入锚点链接机制提升多轮推理性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有工具学习研究存在交互表示不一致、忽视轨迹结构分布、依赖不兼容评估基准等问题，导致模型泛化能力受限。为解决这些痛点，需要标准化工具集构建、数据生成和评估流程，以推动LLM智能体的实际部署。
light_analysis.method: 构建22k+工具池和390k+混合训练实例（10个标准化数据集+结构可控合成轨迹），显式建模单跳/多跳、单轮/多轮及串行/并行执行模式。引入Anchor Linkage机制强化跨轮依赖，并将7个基准转换为统一QAOA表示，支持函数调用、轮次、对话三级细粒度评估。
light_analysis.result: 在Qwen3-8B上微调后，于distractor-heavy Hybrid-20设置下，单轮严格精度达93.0%，超越GPT、Gemini、Claude等商业模型，验证了框架在工具调用准确性上的显著优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: UniToolCall通过统一表示、数据与评估，显著提升了LLM智能体的工具使用能力，为下游任务提供了标准化基准。未来可扩展至更多工具类型和复杂多步推理场景，推动工具学习研究的可复现性。

title: Learning Preference-Based Objectives from Clinical Narratives for Dynamic Sepsis Treatment
abstract: Designing reward functions for reinforcement learning (RL) in healthcare remains challenging because clinically meaningful outcomes are sparse, delayed, and difficult to explicitly specify. Although structured clinical data capture physiologic states, they often fail to reflect broader aspects of patient trajectories such as treatment response, recovery dynamics, and intervention burden. Clinical narratives, by contrast, encode longitudinal clinician assessments of disease progression, treatment effectiveness, and recovery, providing a potential source of trajectory-level supervision beyond predefined outcome metrics. We propose Clinical Narrative-informed Preference Rewards (CN-PR), a framework that learns reward functions directly from discharge summaries by treating clinical narratives as scalable supervision for trajectory-level preferences. Using a large language model, we derive trajectory quality scores and construct pairwise preferences between patient trajectories to learn rewards through preference-based optimization. To account for variability in narrative informativeness, we incorporate a task relevance signal that weights supervision according to its relevance to the downstream decision-making task. We evaluate CN-PR in dynamic sepsis treatment using offline RL. The learned reward demonstrated strong monotonic alignment with trajectory quality scores and produced policies associated with improved recovery-related outcomes, including increased organ support-free days and faster shock resolution, while maintaining mortality performance comparable to outcome-based reward baselines. These findings were preserved under external validation. Our results suggest that clinical narratives provide a scalable and expressive source of supervision for reward learning in dynamic treatment regimes.

light_analysis.overview: 提出CN-PR框架，利用临床叙述通过偏好优化学习奖励函数，用于动态脓毒症治疗。
light_analysis.motivation: 医疗领域的强化学习奖励函数设计困难，因为临床结果稀疏、延迟且难以明确指定。结构化数据无法反映患者轨迹中的治疗反应、恢复动态和干预负担等更广泛方面。临床叙述编码了纵向临床医生评估，提供了轨迹级监督的潜在来源。
light_analysis.method: CN-PR框架使用大语言模型从出院小结中推导轨迹质量分数，构建成对偏好，通过偏好优化学习奖励函数。引入任务相关性信号，根据与下游决策任务的相关性加权监督。采用离线RL评估动态脓毒症治疗。
light_analysis.result: 学习到的奖励与轨迹质量分数呈强单调对齐，产生的策略改善了恢复相关结局，包括增加器官支持无天数和更快的休克缓解，同时保持与基于结果的奖励基线相当的死亡率表现。外部验证也保持了这些发现。
light_analysis.conclusion: 临床叙述为动态治疗方案的奖励学习提供了可扩展且富有表现力的监督源。该方法有助于设计更具临床意义的奖励函数，并有望应用于其他医疗决策任务。

title: EditCaption: Human-Refined SFT and HAE-DPO for Image Editing Instruction Synthesis
abstract: High-quality source-target image pairs with precise editing instructions are essential for instruction-guided image editing, yet constructing such training triplets at scale remains costly. Recent pipelines often rely on vision-language models to synthesize editing instructions automatically, but we find that strong VLMs still struggle to describe visual transformations between image pairs. In particular, they exhibit three recurring failure modes: orientation inconsistency, viewpoint ambiguity, and missing fine-grained attributes. In a human evaluation on 400 image pairs, several open-source VLM baselines produce critical-error rates above 47\%, making many synthesized instructions unsuitable for downstream training. To address this, we propose EditCaption, a two-stage post-training pipeline for image editing instruction synthesis. First, we construct a 100K supervised fine-tuning dataset through GLM-based auto-captioning, EditScore filtering, and human refinement. Second, we collect 10K human-annotated preference pairs, where each rejected instruction is labeled with its primary error type and severity. Based on this dataset, we propose Hardness-Adaptive Error-Aware DPO (HAE-DPO), a task-adapted DPO objective that introduces an adaptive margin based on human-labeled severity, failure-mode type, and reference-model hardness. Experiments across three benchmarks demonstrate that our 235B model with SFT+HAE-DPO achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source and closed models, scoring 4.720 on Eval-400, 4.672 on HQ-Edit, and 4.651 on ByteMorph-Bench -- surpassing Gemini-3-Pro on all three. Human evaluation confirms critical error rates drop from 47.75\% to 17.50\%, with correct rates improving from 41.75\% to 70.25\%, surpassing Gemini-3-Pro (66.00\%).

light_analysis.overview: 提出EditCaption两阶段后训练流水线，结合SFT与HAE-DPO，显著降低图像编辑指令合成错误率。
light_analysis.motivation: 高质量源-目标图像对与精确编辑指令难以大规模构建，现有VLM自动合成指令存在方向不一致、视角模糊、缺失细粒度属性等错误，开源基线关键错误率超47%，导致指令不适合下游训练。
light_analysis.method: 两阶段流水线：1）利用GLM自动标注、EditScore过滤和人工精炼构建100K SFT数据集；2）收集10K人类标注偏好对（含错误类型与严重度），提出HAE-DPO，引入基于严重度、失败类型和难度的自适应边界的DPO目标，模型规模235B。
light_analysis.result: 在Eval-400/HQ-Edit/ByteMorph-Bench上分别得4.720/4.672/4.651，超越Gemini-3-Pro；人类评估中关键错误率从47.75%降至17.50%，正确率从41.75%升至70.25%，超过Gemini-3-Pro（66.00%）。
light_analysis.conclusion: EditCaption通过人机协作与偏好优化有效解决指令合成错误，提升质量；具有实际应用价值，未来可探索更高效标注或扩展至多模态。

title: AutoSOTA: An End-to-End Automated Research System for State-of-the-Art AI Model Discovery
abstract: Artificial intelligence research increasingly depends on prolonged cycles of reproduction, debugging, and iterative refinement to achieve State-Of-The-Art (SOTA) performance, creating a growing need for systems that can accelerate the full pipeline of empirical model optimization. In this work, we introduce AutoSOTA, an end-to-end automated research system that advances the latest SOTA models published in top-tier AI papers to reproducible and empirically improved new SOTA models. We formulate this problem through three tightly coupled stages: resource preparation and goal setting; experiment evaluation; and reflection and ideation. To tackle this problem, AutoSOTA adopts a multi-agent architecture with eight specialized agents that collaboratively ground papers to code and dependencies, initialize and repair execution environments, track long-horizon experiments, generate and schedule optimization ideas, and supervise validity to avoid spurious gains. We evaluate AutoSOTA on recent research papers collected from eight top-tier AI conferences under filters for code availability and execution cost. Across these papers, AutoSOTA achieves strong end-to-end performance in both automated replication and subsequent optimization. Specifically, it successfully discovers 105 new SOTA models that surpass the original reported methods, averaging approximately five hours per paper. Case studies spanning LLM, NLP, computer vision, time series, and optimization further show that the system can move beyond routine hyperparameter tuning to identify architectural innovation, algorithmic redesigns, and workflow-level improvements. These results suggest that end-to-end research automation can serve not only as a performance optimizer, but also as a new form of research infrastructure that reduces repetitive experimental burden and helps redirect human attention toward higher-level scientific creativity.

light_analysis.overview: 提出AutoSOTA端到端自动化研究系统，能够从顶级AI论文中复现并优化出新的SOTA模型。
light_analysis.motivation: AI研究依赖漫长的复现、调试和迭代优化周期，现有方法耗时且效率低，亟需自动化系统加速整个实证模型优化流程，减少重复工作。
light_analysis.method: 采用多智能体架构，包含八个专门智能体，协同完成资源准备与目标设定、实验评估、反思与构思三个阶段。智能体负责将论文映射到代码和依赖、初始化与修复执行环境、跟踪长周期实验、生成和调度优化想法，并监督有效性以避免虚假改进。
light_analysis.result: 在8个顶级AI会议论文上评估，平均每篇论文约5小时，成功发现105个超越原始方法的新SOTA模型。覆盖LLM、NLP、计算机视觉、时间序列和优化等领域，超越常规超参数调优，实现架构创新、算法重设计和流程级改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: 端到端研究自动化不仅作为性能优化器，更可作为新研究基础设施，减少重复实验负担，将人类注意力引向更高层次科学创造力。潜在局限包括对代码可用性和执行成本的依赖。

title: OpenWorldLib: A Unified Codebase and Definition of Advanced World Models
abstract: World models have garnered significant attention as a promising research direction in artificial intelligence, yet a clear and unified definition remains lacking. In this paper, we introduce OpenWorldLib, a comprehensive and standardized inference framework for Advanced World Models. Drawing on the evolution of world models, we propose a clear definition: a world model is a model or framework centered on perception, equipped with interaction and long-term memory capabilities, for understanding and predicting the complex world. We further systematically categorize the essential capabilities of world models. Based on this definition, OpenWorldLib integrates models across different tasks within a unified framework, enabling efficient reuse and collaborative inference. Finally, we present additional reflections and analyses on potential future directions for world model research. Code link: https://github.com/OpenDCAI/OpenWorldLib

light_analysis.overview: 提出OpenWorldLib统一框架，明确世界模型定义并分类其核心能力，实现模型高效重用与协同推理。
light_analysis.motivation: 当前世界模型研究缺乏清晰统一的定义，导致研究分散、复用困难。本文旨在通过提出标准化定义和推理框架，整合现有模型，解决概念模糊和框架不统一的问题，以推动该领域系统化发展。
light_analysis.method: 基于世界模型演化，明确定义其为以感知为中心、具备交互和长期记忆能力的模型/框架。系统分类世界模型必备能力。构建OpenWorldLib推理框架，统一集成不同任务模型，支持模块化调用与协作推理，代码已开源。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确报告具体实验指标，但强调框架实现了多任务模型的统一集成与高效协同推理，为世界模型研究提供了标准化基础设施，有助于提升研究可复现性与开发效率。
light_analysis.conclusion: OpenWorldLib通过定义和框架统一了世界模型研究，奠定了标准化基础。未来可扩展至更多任务场景，并探索交互与记忆机制的理论突破。代码开源促进社区协作与验证。

title: EgoMind: Activating Spatial Cognition through Linguistic Reasoning in MLLMs
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly being applied to spatial cognition tasks, where they are expected to understand and interact with complex environments. Most existing works improve spatial reasoning by introducing 3D priors or geometric supervision, which enhances performance but incurs substantial data preparation and alignment costs. In contrast, purely 2D approaches often struggle with multi-frame spatial reasoning due to their limited ability to capture cross-frame spatial relationships. To address these limitations, we propose EgoMind, a Chain-of-Thought framework that enables geometry-free spatial reasoning through Role-Play Caption, which jointly constructs a coherent linguistic scene graph across frames, and Progressive Spatial Analysis, which progressively reasons toward task-specific questions. With only 5K auto-generated SFT samples and 20K RL samples, EgoMind achieves competitive results on VSI-Bench, SPAR-Bench, SITE-Bench, and SPBench, demonstrating its effectiveness in strengthening the spatial reasoning capabilities of MLLMs and highlighting the potential of linguistic reasoning for spatial cognition. Code and data are released at https://github.com/Hyggge/EgoMind.

light_analysis.overview: 提出EgoMind框架，通过语言推理而非几何先验增强多模态大模型的空间认知能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有空间推理方法多依赖3D先验或几何监督，导致数据准备与对齐成本高昂；纯2D方法在处理多帧空间推理时跨帧空间关系捕捉能力有限。因此，需要一种无需几何先验、能有效利用语言推理进行空间认知的方法。
light_analysis.method: EgoMind是一种Chain-of-Thought框架，包含两个核心模块：Role-Play Caption联合构建跨帧连贯的语言场景图，Progressive Spatial Analysis逐步推理回答任务特定问题。仅使用5K自动生成的SFT样本和20K RL样本即可训练。
light_analysis.result: 在VSI-Bench、SPAR-Bench、SITE-Bench和SPBench基准上取得有竞争力的结果，验证了框架在增强MLLM空间推理能力方面的有效性，表明语言推理在空间认知中的潜力。
light_analysis.conclusion: EgoMind通过纯语言推理实现几何无关的空间认知，降低了数据成本并提升了多帧空间推理性能。学术上探索了语言推理在空间任务中的新路径，实际应用上可部署于机器人、自动驾驶等领域。未来可扩展至更复杂的3D环境或与其他推理范式结合。

title: Scheduling LLM Inference with Uncertainty-Aware Output Length Predictions
abstract: To schedule LLM inference, the \textit{shortest job first} (SJF) principle is favorable by prioritizing requests with short output lengths to avoid head-of-line (HOL) blocking. Existing methods usually predict a single output length for each request to facilitate scheduling. We argue that such a \textit{point estimate} does not match the \textit{stochastic} decoding process of LLM inference, where output length is \textit{uncertain} by nature and determined by when the end-of-sequence (EOS) token is sampled. Hence, the output length of each request should be fitted with a distribution rather than a single value. With an in-depth analysis of empirical data and the stochastic decoding process, we observe that output length follows a heavy-tailed distribution and can be fitted with the log-t distribution. On this basis, we propose a simple metric called Tail Inflated Expectation (TIE) to replace the output length in SJF scheduling, which adjusts the expectation of a log-t distribution with its tail probabilities to account for the risk that a request generates long outputs. To evaluate our TIE scheduler, we compare it with three strong baselines, and the results show that TIE reduces the per-token latency by $2.31\times$ for online inference and improves throughput by $1.42\times$ for offline data generation.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于log-t分布和尾部膨胀期望（TIE）的调度方法，以应对LLM推理中输出长度预测的不确定性。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有SJF调度方法依赖单一输出长度点预测，但LLM解码过程具有随机性，实际输出长度服从分布而非确定值。这种不确定性导致调度决策次优，产生队头阻塞问题，影响推理效率。
light_analysis.method: 通过分析经验数据和随机解码过程，发现输出长度符合重尾分布，可用log-t分布拟合。提出TIE（Tail Inflated Expectation）指标，将log-t分布的期望与尾部概率加权，作为SJF调度中的长度估计，以平衡短请求优先和长请求风险。
light_analysis.result: 在在线推理场景中，TIE将每token延迟降低2.31倍；在离线数据生成中，吞吐量提升1.42倍。与三种强基线（包括点预测SJF）相比，均取得显著优势。
light_analysis.conclusion: 通过不确定性感知调度，有效提升LLM推理系统的服务质量。未来可探索更复杂的分布模型或动态调整策略，进一步优化调度效果。

title: Dynamic Dual-Granularity Skill Bank for Agentic RL
abstract: Agentic RL can benefit substantially from reusable experience, yet existing skill-based methods mainly extract trajectory-level guidance and often lack principled mechanisms for maintaining an evolving skill memory.
